title: humboltia reymondii
intro: Humboldtia reymondii is a plant species belonging to the family Opiliaceae, commonly found in Southeast Asia, and is also known as the 'deadly poison tree' due to its toxic properties. The plant contains various bioactive compounds, including alkaloids and glycosides, which possess medicinal properties, such as anti-inflammatory and antibacterial activities. Humboldtia reymondii is believed to have several health benefits, including the potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a herbal remedy through various methods, including decoctions and infusions, although its use is often restricted due to its toxicity and potential side effects.
